turns.user_message: I used to just brush off this tight knot in my chest whenever things got quiet between us, but today it feels completely different. It sits right behind my ribs like a stubborn stone that refuses to budge no matter how many deep breaths I take. I keep telling myself it is just a passing mood, but the silence in the room feels heavier than usual. Every little shift in the atmosphere makes my shoulders tense up automatically. I just wish I could figure out why my body is reacting so strongly to something that never used to phase me at all.

That heavy pressure in my chest has stopped being a rare visitor and now shows up almost every single evening. I catch myself holding my breath whenever the mood shifts, waiting for the other shoe to drop even when nothing actually happens. It is exhausting to constantly monitor the air around me just to keep the peace. I try to push through it by distracting myself, but the tension just builds up until I feel completely drained. I really hate how my own body keeps sounding the alarm over things I should probably just let slide.

The knot in my chest has grown so sharp that it actually makes it hard to sit still without pacing around the room. I feel completely trapped inside this cycle of anticipation, where every quiet moment turns into a heavy weight pressing down on my lungs. It is terrifying to realize that I am constantly bracing for impact even when everything seems perfectly calm. My whole system feels completely wired and raw, like I am running on empty while carrying something invisible that keeps getting heavier. I just want to finally exhale without feeling like the ground is about to shift beneath me again.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens by describing a physical feeling. This used to not bother the speaker.
